messages.content:
Which group does NOT describe the disturbance regime?
Group of answer choices

Size, agent, severity

Shape, force, return interval

Distribution, frequency, connectivity

All options are correct

Intensity, diversity, predictability
Intensity, diversity, predictability.

Compared to old-growth forests, second-growth forests ________.
Group of answer choices

support less biodiversity than the forests they replace

in North America are mostly in British Columbia and Alaska

are those forests in the National Parks that are protected from logging

are less abundant on Earth today than they were 500 years ago

All options are correct
are less abundant on Earth today than they were 500 years ago.

Considering the development of a forest stand, forests reach their greatest ecological complexity when ________.
Group of answer choices

shrubs and other ground cover plants are absent

they are fully mature with multi-level canopies

they are frequently logged using clear-cutting

they are in the fast growth stage in the early stages of recovering from logging

they are frequently burned
they are fully mature with multi-level canopies.

Why ecosystems are complex adaptive systems?

1Because ecosystems have many parts and they interact with each other
2Because ecosystems have both positive and negative feedbacks
3Because ecosystems are non-linear but equilibrium systems
4Because ecosystems are influenced by external factors
Group of answer choices

1, 2 & 4

2 & 3

1, 3 & 4

1, 2 & 3

2, 3 & 4

1 & 3
1, 2 & 4 are correct.

Which of the following is NOT one of the direct impacts of insect outbreak?
Group of answer choices

Tissue loss

Tree growth reduction

All options are directed impacts of insect outbreak

Pathogen transmission

Tree mortality

Deformation
All options are direct impacts of insect outbreak.

Which of the following is correct?
Group of answer choices

Defoliators will not affect the photosynthetic activities of trees.

All options are correct.

Woodborers can develop in sapwood and are all associated with fungi.

Defoliators prefer young foliage and can feed on old foliage as well.

Mountain pine beetles may not always carry pathogens like fungi.
Mountain pine beetles may not always carry pathogens like fungi.

In the context insect herbivory, injury ALWAYS causes damage:
Group of answer choices

True

False
False.

Which statement is true for simple random sampling?
Group of answer choices

The results will be biased estimation of the mean of the entire population

Samples can be select in order

All options are correct.

Every element has the same chance of being selected

Confidence interval indicates the variation of those estimates
Every element has the same chance of being selected.

What is PSP?

1PSP stands for permanent sample plots
2PSP is often used to develop models of different growth rate from different land
3PSP is used to monitor a continuous forest inventory
Group of answer choices

1 & 2

1 & 2 & 3

1 & 3

2 & 3
1 & 3 are correct.

Forests in Canada do NOT include ________ forests.
Group of answer choices

subalpine

deciduous

old-growth

second-growth

mangrove
Mangrove.

Which of the following criterion is NOT aligned with the Montréal Process?
Group of answer choices

All criteria fall within the agreement.

Logging restrictions should be applied when the activities violated the corresponding legislation in countries of harvest.

Ecosystem productivity, health, and vitality are important facets in forest maintenance.

Societal demands need to be fully considered.

Institutional framework of conservation should be improved to maintain the sustainability of forest management.

The role of forests in carbon cycles need to be maintained in the long term.

Ecosystem services provided by forests (e.g. biodiversity, soil protection, etc.) need to conserved.
Logging restrictions should be applied when the activities violated the corresponding legislation in countries of harvest.

Which of the following statement about plantation forests is correct?
Group of answer choices

Tree plantations can be adopted in areas experiencing groundwater depletion.

Plantation forests can improve the nutrient availability, thus not degrading the soil quality.

Planted trees may alter the salinity and acidity of the freshwater due to the accumulation of pollutants.

Tree plantations are unlikely to diminish the productivity of natural forests as they are managed differently.

None is correct.

Plantation forests can increase the revenue for forest-dependent communities, thus bringing socio-economic benefits only.
Tree plantations can increase the revenue for forest-dependent communities, thus bringing socio-economic benefits only.

Which of the following statement regarding natural forests is incorrect?
Group of answer choices

Most of the natural forested areas are under legal restrictions for protection.

Natural forests are experiencing habitat fragmentation as well as habitat loss in recent years.

All options are correct.

Fragmented habitats in natural forests are degrading over time.

There are more semi-natural forests than undisturbed ones.

Timber harvesting can pose great threats to natural forests in many nations.

Forest loss is one of the major concerns for natural forests worldwide.
All options except "There are more semi-natural forests than undisturbed ones" are correct. There are more undisturbed forests than semi-natural ones.
Canadian forests approximately constitute ________% of Earth's forested areas.
Canadian forests approximately constitute 10% of Earth's forested areas.

Salvage logging (i.e. removing damaged trees after the wildfire, storms, or insect outbreaks) does NOT ________.
Group of answer choices

help the plantation in burned forests

create uneven age forest stands

make sense economically in the short term

None is correct

reduce the number of habitats for animals、
Help the plantation in burned forests.

Which of the following is NOT true of scenario planning?
Group of answer choices

None is correct.

The use of computer simulation models can help generate scenarios.

It allows for the risk and uncertainty.

Adjustment of the planning is unnecessary when the management objectives are not met.

We can use scenario planning can assess future outcomes.

We need to assess how well our management assumptions and priorities meet the objectives.
Adjustment of the planning is unnecessary when the management objectives are not met.

Which of the following is NOT the strategy of measuring risk and uncertainty?
Group of answer choices

Sensitivity analysis of assumptions

All options are correct.

Higher inventory of growing stock

Focus on some specific species

Higher investment in prevention
Higher inventory of growing stock.

Which of the following is a type of forest management that allows for uneven-aged stand management?
Group of answer choices

Clear-cutting

Selection systems

Shelterwood

None is correct

Seed-tree approach
Selection systems.

Biodiversity conservation may help improve food security because ________.
Group of answer choices

it decreases the number of available pathogens that may weaken crops

it decreases the number of potential predators that may feed on major crops

None is correct.

it decreases the number of pollinators that may help crops reproduce

it increases the genetic diversity that may benefit crops
It increases the genetic diversity that may benefit crops.

Which statement is NOT true for the Conservation Prioritization (CP)?
Group of answer choices

CP can give the best approach for management

CP is a method for decision making

CP is a method used for ecosystem at risk

CP can forecast the long-term results
CP can forecast the long-term results.

What has become the most effective way to achieve compliance for conservation planning?
Group of answer choices

Imposing hefty fines on infractors

None of the above

All of the above

Jailing those who violate or bend the rules

Gathering all interested parties to discuss and decide on a course of action
Gathering all interested parties to discuss and decide on a course of action.

Which of the following statement is NOT true?
Group of answer choices

GIS is about formatting and analyzing the geographic and geospatial information in map forms.

GIS needs to incorporate databases with spatial information in an integrated system.

All options are true.

Only a few GIS projects do not need a large amount of time for data collection and database development.

GIS can tell us about what the place has and where the place is, but not when the place was studied.

GIS is a computer-based system that involves people and data.
All options are true.

Which of the following statement is true?
Group of answer choices

Hydrological analysis need the FORCAST model to evaluate streams and buffers.

Both harvest modelling and yield predictions rely on the model of SIMFOR.

None is correct.

GIS cannot determine the operability of a forest stand due to its complexity.

The suitability of a habitat can be informed by the 3D driver simulation.

All GIS models are wrong due to their simple representations of the reality.

No GIS model can help the studies of genetic diversity.
The suitability of a habitat can be informed by the 3D driver simulation.

Which of the following statement is incorrect?
Group of answer choices

Vector data, in general, uses groups of coordinates to represent realities.

Lines are ordered sets of coordinates which must have starting and ending points.

Point data can represent a pair of coordinates, thus not showing the dimension.

Geoprocessing is the process that uses different tools and models to manage GIS data.

Each polygon should have an interior region and non-shared boundaries.

All options are correct.
All options except "Each polygon should have an interior region and non-shared boundaries" are correct, polygons can have shared boundaries.

The time required for a seed to start germinating is known as:
Group of answer choices

Emergence

Dormancy

Imbibition

Sprouting

Establishment
Imbibition.

__________ is the change in leaf color
Group of answer choices

Chlorosis

Pigmentation

Resinosis

Hypertrophy

Necrosis
Chlorosis.

Which is NOT true for rust?
Group of answer choices

Exotic rust cannot be devastating

Heteroecious rusts must alternate on different hosts

Domestic rust can be prevented by genetic method

None is correct.

Rust is one of the most destructive group of plant pathogens
Exotic rust cannot be devastating is not true for rust. Rust can be devastating whether it is native or exotic.

Which statement is NOT true for fungi?
Group of answer choices

It is easier for beetle to reproduce on fungi

None is correct.

Fungi can be the food for wildlife

Fungi can decompose cellulose but not lignin

Fungi participates in carbon and nutrient cycling
Fungi can decompose cellulose but not lignin is not true for fungi.

Which statement is NOT true for a healthy forest?
Group of answer choices

Canker disease often happens in the old trees

Human can impact the forest health by bringing exotic pathogens

The special scale can influence the evaluation criteria

There are different criteria from different aspect

None is correct.
The special scale can influence the evaluation criteria. (It should be "spatial scale")

Which of the following may NOT be used to differentiate between remote sensing systems and the data they collect?
Group of answer choices

All options can be used to differentiate remote sensing systems and their data collection.

Radiometric resolution

Sources of energy (e.g. passive vs. active)

Spatial resolution

Swath

Platform type (e.g. ground-based, vs., airborne, vs. spaceborne)
All options can be used to differentiate remote sensing systems and their data collection.

What remote sensing dataset would be most appropriate for studying the seasonal patterns of vegetation for the whole planet over a single year?
Group of answer choices

RADAR

Both Landsat and IKONOS

Google Earth

Landsat

MERIS

IKONOS

MODIS

LiDAR
MODIS.

Which remote sensing data type would be best for locating flooded areas underneath a forest canopy?
Group of answer choices

Landsat

MERIS

MODIS

Google Earth

UAV

LiDAR

Both Landsat and MODIS

RADAR
RADAR.

Which of the following statement is false?
Group of answer choices

All options are correct.

Remote sensing measurements can be taken from centimetres to kilometres.

Vegetation and dark soil can be separated via Landsat multispectral scanner at 2000 nm wavelength.

Turbid water and dark soil may be differentiated by Landsat multispectral scanner from the 800 to 900 nm wavelength.

Nectar in flowers may be seen as dark blue areas for honey bees.

Human eyes can only see a small proportion of the electromagnetic spectrum, especially the visible section.

Photography was invented before aerial images were taken from balloons and birds.
Vegetation and dark soil can be separated via Landsat multispectral scanner at 2000 nm wavelength. (It should be "1400-1500 nm wavelength")

Which of the following statement is correct?
Group of answer choices

Mean annual increment (MAI) can be calculated as stand age divided by stand volume.

Culmination age can be used as the optimal rotation age to maximize the long-term timber production for a forest stand.

All options are correct.

Culmination age is the age at which a forest stand reaches its largest volume.

Mean annual increment (MAI) cannot be used to evaluate the site productivity (e.g. merchantable timber volume) of a forest stand.
Culmination age can be used as the optimal rotation age to maximize the long-term timber production for a forest stand.

Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the old-growth stage of a forest stand?
Group of answer choices

The old-growth forest can include both climax forests and sub-climax forests.

The age and structure of old-growth forests may vary significantly by forest types.

Old-growth forests contain both living and dead trees of different sizes, species, and structures.

As the old-growth forest is a dynamic ecosystem, it can still slowly change over time.

None is correct.
None is correct.

Which of the following may NOT be used as one of the stand attributes (e.g. structural characteristics) for old-growth forests?
Group of answer choices

Understory patchiness and canopy gaps

All factors are correct.

Accumulation of large, dead, standing, and fallen trees

Variation of tree ages, sizes, and spacing

Multiple canopy layers

Growth rate of dominant trees
Growth rate of dominant trees.

The amount of timber produced by a specific forest stand over a certain period of time can be defined as 
 .

The increase in certain tree attributes over a certain period of time can be defined as 
 .

The amount of timber produced by a specific forest stand over a certain period of time can be defined as yield or volume.

The increase in certain tree attributes over a certain period of time can be defined as increment.
The increase in certain tree attributes over a certain period of time can be defined as
Increment.

Which of the following is incorrect?
Group of answer choices

In climate visioning models, vegetation communities are likely to move with the climate zone.

Climate change can directly influence species distributions and tree regeneration success.

Climate models play an important role in understanding and visualizing the impacts of climate change.

Climate change can indirectly affect forest dynamics as well as fire regimes.

All options are correct.

Scientific terms should not be overused in climate change communications.

Future climate conditions may differ from current or past conditions.
All options are correct.

The atmosphere on Earth is warmed because  ________.
Group of answer choices

None is correct

molecules in the atmosphere can absorb radiation from Earth

vegetation can absorb CO2

fossil fuels release heat during burning

vegetation can release CO2
Molecules in the atmosphere can absorb radiation from Earth.

Global temperature will increase due to climate warming. Which of the following is wrong?

 

Group of answer choices

The extent of temperate forests can expand in Russia and Canada.

The land will warm up more than the ocean.

None is correct.

Sea level will rise because of the melting of ice caps and glaciers.

The highest increase in temperature will be around Antarctica and Arctic regions.
The extent of temperate forests can expand in Russia and Canada is wrong.

The strategies and actions to minimize, reduce, or moderate a certain force that contributes to climate change can be called 
 . 

The strategies and actions to cope with undesired climate warming scenarios and to improve beneficial responses can be called

The strategies and actions to minimize, reduce, or moderate a certain force that contributes to climate change can be called mitigation.

The strategies and actions to cope with undesired climate warming scenarios and to improve beneficial responses can be called adaptation.

Which of the following statement is false?
Group of answer choices

Localize, spatialize, and visualize are important stages of the climate visioning process.

Risk assessment is needed for evaluating adaptation plans and policy recommendations.

Community consultation usually should not be executed after localize or spatialize.

No option is false.

The scenario of "deep sustainability" can be achievable without a huge cost in most cases.

The scenario of "adapt to risk" may lead to the same result as the scenario of "do nothing".
The scenario of "adapt to risk" may lead to the same result as the scenario of "do nothing" is false.

Which of the following statement is false?
Group of answer choices

Localize, spatialize, and visualize are important stages of the climate visioning process.

Risk assessment is needed for evaluating adaptation plans and policy recommendations.

Community consultation usually should not be executed after localize or spatialize.

No option is false.

The scenario of "deep sustainability" can be achievable without a huge cost in most cases.

The scenario of "adapt to risk" may lead to the same result as the scenario of "do nothing".
Community consultation usually should not be executed after localize or spatialize. (It should be "Community consultation should be executed after localize and spatialize.")
